mentions.note: HCM/finance software, down 43%; 18% growth, <15x P/E; name-brand quality at deep discount.
Enterprise software hit on AI coding threat; clients may demand shorter contracts and lower price increases given AI alternatives; multiple compression real even if fundamentals intact.

Down 12 points on Tuesday (130 to 117), caught two downgrades and price-target cuts due to Anthropic AI threat narrative. Rallied +3 yesterday, +22 points from yesterday close today. Program buying lifted stock despite dismal guidance. Cramer sees program-driven rebound, not fundamental improvement.

Fifth-worst performer (−23.8%); enterprise software + CEO transition to co-founder Aneel Bhusri; lowered full-year guidance 3 months after prior forecast; now trades at 13x forward earnings but probably too soon to bottom-fish.
Up 7%+ despite disappointing quarter; new CEO (predecessor of prior CEO); market repositioning on fundamentals.
Enterprise software rebound; temporary reprieve but justified given fundamental pivoting to stay relevant in AI era.
HCM and ERP vendor under pressure as AI agents and OpenAI's Frontier model enable custom workflows without complex platform lock-in.

Enterprise software for finance/HCM down 40% Q1; double-strike (enterprise software + AI→layoffs); Cramer tried to be hero Jan, immediately CEO Carl Eschenbach stepped down, mixed quarter reported; learned lesson not to fight displacement here. Too risky.
Down 6.5% due to AI fears. SaaS company whose software-per-worker model threatened by AI headcount reduction. Cramer questions existential risk but understands narrative.
Beloved SaaS but poster boy for AI displacement; rallied 5% today as part of software rotation but likely short squeeze, not sustainable.
